dashboard.label: Setup
dashboard.description: Configure MITRE ATLAS AI Threat Detection for your environment

input.label: Index to scan (e.g. main, ai_logs, or * for all)

div.p: The atlas_index macro controls which index all 10 detection rules query. Update it to match your environment.
How to update: Settings → Advanced Search → Search Macros → atlas_index

search.query: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/gic-atlas-detections/configs/conf-macros/atlas_index | table title, definition | rename title AS "Macro Name", definition AS "Current Definition"

panel.title: Step 2: Discover AI/LLM Data in Your Environment

div.p: Click Submit above to scan your indexes for known AI/LLM sourcetypes. If you see results below, you have data the detection rules can use.

search.query: | tstats count where index=$scan_index$ sourcetype IN ( "llm_gateway", "openai_logs", "azure_openai", "bedrock_logs", "rag_logs", "agent_logs", "api_gateway", "aws:s3:access", "azure:storage:blob", "gcp:storage", "mlflow_logs", "dvc_logs", "dataset_registry", "content_safety", "moderations_api", "okta", "azure:aad", "web:access", "nginx:access", "apache:access", "cloudflare:access", "litellm_proxy", "litellm:proxy", "litellm", "azure:monitor:resource", "aws:cloudwatch:logs", "kong:ai", "portkey", "helicone" ) by index, sourcetype | rename count AS "Event Count", index AS "Index", sourcetype AS "Sourcetype" | sort - "Event Count"

div.p: This checks whether your data contains the fields the detection rules need. Rules are grouped into two tiers:

Tier 1 — Operational (works with standard telemetry): Token counts, API call volumes, storage access logs. Rules: Exfiltration (T0024), Bulk Abuse (T0047), Artifact Discovery (T0007), Training Data Poisoning (T0020), Valid Account Abuse (T0012), Safety Flag (T0048).

Tier 2 — Content Inspection (requires prompt logging): Prompt text and LLM responses. Rules: Direct Prompt Injection (T0051.000), Indirect Prompt Injection (T0051.001), Jailbreak (T0054), Model Reconnaissance (T0014).

search.query: index=$scan_index$ sourcetype IN ( "llm_gateway", "openai_logs", "azure_openai", "bedrock_logs", "api_gateway", "aws:s3:access", "azure:storage:blob", "gcp:storage", "litellm_proxy", "litellm:proxy", "litellm", "azure:monitor:resource", "aws:cloudwatch:logs", "okta", "azure:aad", "rag_logs", "agent_logs", "content_safety", "moderations_api" ) | head 1000 | fieldsummary | where field IN ("user", "src_ip", "total_tokens", "prompt_tokens", "completion_tokens", "model", "session_id", "event_type", "action", "object_key", "uri_path", "http_status", "safety_flagged", "finish_reason") | eval status=if(count>0, "Found (".count." events)", "Not Found") | table field, count, distinct_count, status | rename field AS "Field Name", count AS "Events with Field", distinct_count AS "Unique Values", status AS "Status"

index=$scan_index$ sourcetype IN ( "llm_gateway", "openai_logs", "azure_openai", "bedrock_logs", "rag_logs", "agent_logs", "litellm_proxy", "litellm:proxy", "litellm", "content_safety", "moderations_api" ) | head 1000 | fieldsummary | where field IN ("user_input", "prompt", "request_text", "message", "messages", "retrieved_content", "document_text", "tool_output", "fetched_text", "response", "completion") | eval status=if(count>0, "Found (".count." events)", "Not Found") | table field, count, distinct_count, status | rename field AS "Field Name", count AS "Events with Field", distinct_count AS "Unique Values", status AS "Status"

tr.td: Operational — needs token counts and user fields
Operational — needs token counts and event_type
Operational — needs storage access logs (S3/Azure Blob/GCS)
Operational — needs storage upload logs with object paths
Operational — needs user and IP geolocation data
Operational — needs safety_flagged or finish_reason fields
Content — needs user_input or prompt text
Content — needs user_input or prompt text
Content — needs user_input or prompt text

div.p: After you have confirmed your data and updated the atlas_index macro:

ol.li: Go to Settings → Searches, Reports, and Alerts
Set App filter to MITRE ATLAS AI Threat Detection for Splunk
Enable the rules that match your available data tier

div.p: All rules ship disabled by default. Enabled rules run on their configured schedule (every 15–30 minutes) and write detection results to the summary index. The Detections dashboard reads from the summary index.
For platform-specific setup instructions to get AI/LLM data into Splunk, see the Configuration Guide tab.

search.query: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/gic-atlas-detections/saved/searches | where match(title, "^ATLAS") | eval status=if(disabled=0, "Enabled", "Disabled") | eval schedule=if(disabled=0, cron_schedule, "N/A — disabled") | table title, status, schedule | rename title AS "Rule", status AS "Status", schedule AS "Schedule" | sort Rule
